D2 Vision L3 Apply Reference

LMS Cone Sensitivity Curves

see the three overlapping cone curves that turn a spectrum into three numbers

The retina holds three cone types — L, M, and S — whose spectral sensitivities overlap so heavily that any light collapses to just three responses.

Slide the wavelength marker across the spectrum. Read where it cuts each curve to get the L, M, and S responses, and notice how tightly the L and M peaks sit together — the root of red–green colour vision.
